confusion_points.description: The email placeholder text said 'you@organization.org' which made me briefly wonder if this site was only for organisations and whether my regular personal email would be accepted

There was no confirmation pop-up or review screen before the account was created — clicking 'Create account' immediately submitted the form. I would have expected a 'Please review your details' step or at least a confirmation dialog before committing

suggestions: Change the email placeholder from 'you@organization.org' to something more generic like 'you@example.com' so personal users don't feel excluded
Add a brief confirmation step or success message that clearly says 'Account created! Welcome, Susan' rather than jumping straight to Account Settings — it felt a bit abrupt
Consider showing a welcome/onboarding screen after sign-up rather than going directly to Account Settings, which felt a bit technical for a first-time user
reasoning:
The account has been created successfully! I can see I'm now logged in as "Susan" — there's a green "S" avatar next to my name in the top right corner, and the page shows "Account Settings" with all my details filled in:
- Email: susan.miller@email.com
- First name: Susan
- Last name: Miller
- Member since: 3/31/2026

The registration worked perfectly. I can also see options for Dashboard, Browse, My Tickets, and Create Event in the navigation bar — I'm fully logged in.

I did notice one small thing — the email placeholder said "you@organization.org" which briefly made me wonder if the site was only for organisations, but it accepted my regular email just fine. There was also no separate confirmation pop-up before the account was created, which surprised me slightly — but the form itself with the "Create account" button acted as the final step.
